-  "plannerOutput": "## Feature brief — Binayah Ultimate production upgrades (5 stages)

### Overall approach
All 5 features are additive — no existing functionality is removed except the dead 7-step Onboarding flow. Each stage maps to one feature and ships independently. Auth is next-auth v4 with `fixedUserId` on session. All components use inline `t.*` theme tokens (no CSS vars). Lazy-load new heavy panels with `next/dynamic({ ssr: false })`.

---

### Stage 1 — Onboarding redesign

**Remove:** The 7-step `Onboarding.tsx` flow including user-selection steps (steps 1–5), theme/pipeline selection steps. The auth session already identifies the user.

**Add:** A one-time welcome modal (`WelcomeModal.tsx`) that fires on first login (tracked in localStorage key `binayah_welcomed_<fixedUserId>`). The modal is a single-screen experience:
- Greets the user by name (from session / USERS_DEFAULT lookup by fixedUserId)
- Offers inline avatar selection (emoji picker + AI generation — reuse `AvatarStep6` logic stripped from Onboarding.tsx)
- A single CTA: "Enter the war room" (or equivalent per theme)
- Animated background (reuse `FloatingBg` or equivalent)
- Skippable — clicking outside or pressing Escape dismisses and sets the localStorage flag

**Keep:** `AvatarStep6` export (used elsewhere potentially), `FloatingBg` if reused.

**Dashboard changes:** Replace `onboardStep < 7` gate with `!welcomeSeen` check. After modal closes, set `binayah_welcomed_<fixedUserId>` in localStorage.

**PROJECT_CONTEXT notes:**
- Auth session provides `fixedUserId` via next-auth v4 (src/types/next-auth.d.ts). `USERS_DEFAULT` is source of truth for user display data (name, role, color) — only avatar/aiAvatar come from localStorage (hydrateUsers pattern).
- QA bypass: set `binayah_welcomed_<fixedUserId>` in localStorage before navigating to skip modal.
- No `SCHEMA_VERSION` bump needed — new key is additive.

**QA bar:** Modal appears on fresh localStorage, does not appear on second load. Avatar selection persists. TypeScript strict passes. No flash of onboarding on return visits.

---

### Stage 2 — In-app notification bell

**New component:** `NotificationBell.tsx` — renders in Dashboard header (right side, near user stats).

**Bell badge:** Shows count of unseen notifications. Badge is hidden when count = 0.

**Dropdown:** Opens on click, shows last 20 activity entries of type `claimed`, `active`, `comment`. Each row shows: user avatar, action text, stage name, relative timestamp.

**Data source (CONFIRMED: SSE via chatBus — NOT polling):**
- Extend `src/lib/chatBus.ts` to also emit activity events (alongside chat messages). The chatBus EventEmitter already has `setMaxListeners(200)`.
- In `/api/pipeline-state/activity/route.ts`, after writing to MongoDB, emit to chatBus: `chatBus.emit("activity", { type, user, stage, ts, ... })`.
- In `/api/pipeline-state/messages/stream/route.ts` (Node.js runtime, the existing SSE stream), subscribe to both `"message"` and `"activity"` events on chatBus. Send activity events as SSE events with `event: activity` type.
- `NotificationBell.tsx` subscribes to the existing `/api/pipeline-state/messages/stream` SSE endpoint. On receiving `event: activity` events, append to local notification list.
- Gap-fill on connect: fetch `/api/pipeline-state` once on mount to hydrate recent activityLog entries (filtered to claimed + active + comment types).

**Event types shown:** `claimed`, `active`, `comment` only. Lock/unlock and system events are excluded from the bell.

**Seen state:** Clicking the bell marks all as seen (updates `binayah_notif_seen_at` timestamp in localStorage to `Date.now()`). Badge count is derived from entries with `ts > binayah_notif_seen_at`.

**PROJECT_CONTEXT notes:**
- chatBus runtime is `"nodejs"` — stream route already exports `runtime = "nodejs"`. Do NOT change this.
- Subscribe-before-flush order must be maintained — register `chatBus.on` BEFORE the MongoDB gap-fill query (already established pattern in stream route).
- Keep-alive ping is already present (30s). No changes needed there.
- The existing SSE connection is already open in ChatPanel when team-chat tab is active — NotificationBell creates its own separate EventSource subscription to the same endpoint (two subscribers is fine with setMaxListeners(200)).

**QA bar:** Badge count reflects new activity events in real time via SSE. Clicking bell clears badge. Dropdown items render with correct user avatars and relative timestamps. Theming consistent with rest of header.

---

### Stage 3 — Cmd+K global search palette

**New component:** `SearchPalette.tsx` — full-screen dimmed overlay with centered modal input.

**Trigger:** `Cmd+K` (Mac) / `Ctrl+K` (Windows/Linux). Global `keydown` listener in Dashboard.tsx.

**Search scope (CONFIRMED: full content search, all client-side):**
- Stage names (from `pipelineData` — all stages across all pipelines, with pipeline name as context)
- Document titles AND full document content (TipTap JSON body text) — fetch all docs with content on Cmd+K open (`GET /api/documents` does NOT return content in list view, so a fresh `GET /api/documents/[id]` per doc may be needed, or a new `?includeContent=true` query param — DEV to choose most efficient approach)
- Team members (from `USERS_DEFAULT` — 6 users, name + role)
- Client-side fuzzy search in memory. No MongoDB text index.

**Document content fetch strategy:** On palette open, fetch all docs list (`GET /api/documents`), then batch-fetch content for each. Cache results in module-scope ref so re-opens don't refetch until stale (5 min TTL or on doc save). This is an async operation — show a loading state in the palette while docs are fetching.

**Results format (Linear-style):**
- Grouped sections: Stages / Documents / People
- Keyboard navigation (arrow keys + Enter)
- Clicking/entering a stage result navigates to that pipeline and expands the stage
- Clicking a document result opens DocumentsPanel with that doc active
- Clicking a person result opens a user profile or highlights their claims

**Design:** Dark modal, `t.bgCard` surface, `t.border` outline, `t.accent` highlighted match text. `t.shadowLg` drop shadow. Escape closes.

**PROJECT_CONTEXT notes:**
- Documents state is isolated in `DocumentsPanel.tsx` — SearchPalette cannot read it directly. Pass an `onOpenDocument(id)` callback from Dashboard down to SearchPalette, which triggers nav to documents panel and sets active doc.
- `/api/documents` list endpoint does NOT include `content` field in list response (only title, pipelineId, timestamps) — confirm in route before assuming content is available.
- Auth-gated endpoint — SSE connection already means user is logged in; no special handling needed.

**QA bar:** Cmd+K opens palette. Typing filters results including document content. Arrow keys navigate. Enter on a stage result scrolls to/highlights the stage. Escape closes. No TypeScript errors.

---

### Stage 4 — Document collaboration (presence + last-write-wins)

**Approach:** Last-write-wins with last-saved-by attribution only. No Yjs/Hocuspocus (Vercel serverless constraint). No live presence polling.

**Changes to `DocumentsPanel.tsx`:**
- Auto-save on debounce (3s after last keystroke) — already saves on blur; change to also save on debounce
- Show 'Last saved by [name] at [time]' below the editor title, sourced from document `updatedAt` + `updatedBy` fields
- Add `updatedBy` field to `BinayahDocument` schema — set on every PATCH to `session.fixedUserId`
- Show an avatar of the last editor next to the save status line (use `AvatarC` from `src/components/ui/Avatar.tsx`)

**No new SSE endpoint.** No presence heartbeat. No conflict resolution UI.

**API change:** PATCH `/api/documents/[id]` sets `updatedBy = session.user.fixedUserId` server-side (NOT from client body — security). Add `updatedBy` to `BinayahDocument` schema as `String` field.

**PROJECT_CONTEXT notes:**
- Documents API has its own inline whitelist (NOT subject to `validatePatchKeys`). The route already reads session — set `updatedBy` server-side from session.
- `BinayahDocument` model is in `src/lib/BinayahDocument.ts`. Currently: title, content (Mixed), createdBy, pipelineId, timestamps. Add `updatedBy: String`.
- `USERS_DEFAULT` is source of truth for name display — look up `updatedBy` (a fixedUserId) in USERS_DEFAULT to display the name.
- Do NOT add TipTap extensions without also adding theme overrides (PROJECT_CONTEXT constraint).

**QA bar:** After saving a document, 'Last saved by [name]' appears with correct user avatar. Auto-save fires after 3s of inactivity. `updatedBy` stored in MongoDB. TypeScript strict passes.

---

### Stage 5 — Pipeline timeline / burndown

**Location:** New tab/section inside `OverviewPanel.tsx` — toggle between existing metrics view and new timeline view.

**Data source:** `state.activityLog` (fetched with the rest of pipeline state on mount). Filter to `type === 'status_change'` events.

**Prerequisite (CONFIRMED: add status_change logging):** Stage 5 DEV must also patch `PATCH /api/pipeline-state` handler to emit a `status_change` activity entry when `stageStatusOverrides` keys are updated. Entry shape: `{ type: 'status_change', user, stage, from, to, ts }`. Use the existing `POST /api/pipeline-state/activity` pattern (or emit directly in the PATCH handler after persisting). Note: this also means chatBus should emit the activity event (per Stage 2 changes) so the notification bell shows status changes — but status_change is NOT in the bell's filter list (claimed + active + comment only), so no bell display change needed.

**Timeline UI:** Per-pipeline vertical timeline of status changes, ordered by `ts` descending. Each entry shows: stage name, old status → new status (with status color tokens), user avatar, relative time.

**Burndown chart (stretch):** If time permits, a simple SVG/canvas line chart showing count of `active` stages over calendar days. Use only if the activityLog data is rich enough — otherwise skip and ship timeline only.

**PROJECT_CONTEXT notes:**
- `STATUS_ORDER = ["concept", "planned", "in-progress", "active"]` — use these exact strings for status color mapping.
- `activityLog` is capped at 200 entries ($slice: 200, prepended). Pipeline field on each activity entry may not exist — DEV should include `pipeline` in the `status_change` event shape for timeline grouping: `{ type: 'status_change', user, stage, pipeline, from, to, ts }`.
- OverviewPanel is lazy-loaded with `next/dynamic`. Keep this pattern — do not turn it into a server component.
- PATCH key whitelist in `validate.ts` — `stageStatusOverrides` is already whitelisted. No changes needed there.

**QA bar:** Timeline tab shows entries for any pipeline that has had status changes. Status color tokens match the canonical status→color mapping. Entries ordered newest-first. TypeScript strict passes.

### Out of scope (explicitly excluded)

- DLD API integration (legal hold — do not enable)
- New team member additions (no ADMIN_EMAIL_MAP changes)
- Yjs/Hocuspocus/WebSocket server (serverless constraint)
- MongoDB text index for search
- Polling-based notifications (superseded by SSE approach)
- Burndown chart (stretch only — ship timeline first)

### Why this will be good
The activity log already captures most of the story — but status transitions (`concept → planned → in-progress → active`) are the heartbeat of the pipeline, and right now they're not being logged. Adding `status_change` events to the activity log, then rendering them as a per-pipeline vertical timeline, turns the overview panel from a 'metrics dashboard' into a 'what's actually happening' view. Production-ready: writes the log on every override change, renders newest-first, honors canonical status colors, degrades gracefully when a pipeline has no history, lazy-loaded like the rest of OverviewPanel, works in all four themes. Burndown chart is a stretch — ship timeline first. Benchmark: Linear's issue history / Stripe dashboard event log — compact, color-coded transitions, timestamps always present.

### Scope
**Prerequisite (critical, DO NOT skip):** server-side emission of `status_change` activity entries.
- In `PATCH /api/pipeline-state/route.ts`, when `stageStatusOverrides` keys are added/changed, emit one activity entry per change with shape: `{ type: 'status_change', user, stage, pipeline, from, to, ts }`.
- Use the existing `POST /api/pipeline-state/activity` pattern OR write directly after persisting the PATCH — whichever keeps the existing locking / 423 semantics intact.
- Also emit to chatBus (per Stage 2's extension) so the stream carries status_change. NotificationBell filter already excludes status_change so the bell does not display these — correct by design.

**Timeline UI in `OverviewPanel.tsx`:**
- Add a toggle (tabs or segmented control) between existing metrics view and new timeline view. Both should feel equally native to the panel — not 'new tab bolted on'.
- Timeline is per-pipeline (use the currently selected pipeline context). Each entry: stage name + old status → new status (with canonical status color swatches) + actor avatar + relative time.
- Ordered newest-first. Vertical rail with hairline connector between entries (command center, not a bullet list).
- Empty state for pipelines with no status-change history — framed as opportunity per DESIGN_PRINCIPLES §States ('no moves yet — stake something'), in the product's voice.

**Stretch (only if cheap):** a simple SVG line chart of `active`-stage count over calendar days, derived from the same `status_change` stream. Skip if data is thin or it balloons the diff — ship timeline first.

### Intent & quality bar
- **Status colors are canon** (DESIGN_SYSTEM §Status→color mapping and DESIGN_PRINCIPLES §Visual):
  - `concept` → `t.textMuted` · `planned` → `t.amber` · `in-progress` → `t.cyan` · `active` → `t.green`.
  - The 'from → to' transition visualization must use these tokens precisely. Breaking this breaks the language.
- **Signal density is a virtue.** Pack stage name, transition arrow with two color swatches, avatar, relative time into one compact row. Don't over-whitespace.
- **Avatar stack patterns** in DESIGN_SYSTEM apply — reuse `AvatarC`.
- **Ownership is visible** — every timeline row names and pictures the actor.
- **Hierarchy is earned** — pipeline name/context sits at the top; timeline rows are secondary; meta (timestamp) is tertiary.

### Files to touch

- `src/components/OverviewPanel.tsx` (add timeline view + toggle; consume `state.activityLog`)
- (If status_change emission needs a helper) `src/lib/chatBus.ts` or an activity-emit utility — DEV chooses.

### Contract & state
- Data source: `state.activityLog` (already fetched with pipeline state on mount). Filter to `type === 'status_change'` and to the selected pipeline.
- `activityLog` is MongoDB-$sliced to 200 entries (prepended). The timeline shows what's in that window — do NOT add a separate paginated history endpoint in this stage.
- The `pipeline` field must be included in the emitted entry so the timeline can group by selected pipeline (existing activity entries may lack this field — that's fine, old entries won't appear in the timeline and that's acceptable).
- PATCH key whitelist already allows `stageStatusOverrides` (`validate.ts`). No changes there.
- OverviewPanel stays lazy-loaded via `next/dynamic({ ssr: false })`. Do NOT convert to a server component.
- `STATUS_ORDER = ["concept", "planned", "in-progress", "active"]` — use these exact strings.

### Forbidden
- Do NOT add a new API route for timeline data — consume `state.activityLog`.
- Do NOT rename status values or add variants.
- Do NOT convert OverviewPanel to SSR.
- Do NOT show status_change events in the NotificationBell (Stage 2 filter excludes them — keep it that way).
- Do NOT hardcode hex colors. Status color tokens only.
- Do NOT ship the burndown chart if it pushes the diff past reasonable review size — timeline is the priority.

### Acceptance criteria
- After toggling a stage's status in any pipeline, a `status_change` entry appears in the new timeline view for that pipeline, newest-first.
- From/to status color swatches match the canonical mapping exactly.
- Actor avatar + relative time render on every row.
- Pipelines with no status-change history show a themed empty state, not a blank panel.
- Toggle between metrics and timeline is smooth; state preserved on switch.
- TypeScript strict passes.
- All four themes render correctly.

### Demo path
1. Open OverviewPanel for any pipeline. 2. Toggle to Timeline view — see existing status_change entries (or a framed empty state if none). 3. In another panel, change a stage's status from `planned` to `in-progress`. 4. Return to OverviewPanel timeline — the new transition appears at the top with amber → cyan swatches, your avatar, 'just now'. 5. Change status again, see it stack. 6. +      "spec": "## Stage 3a — Batch C Part 1: context extraction + Dashboard split

### Why this will be good
Dashboard.tsx is 2068 lines. This stage extracts pure-UI state into EphemeralContext, all model state + handlers into ModelContext, and splits the render tree into named view components. After this stage, Stage.tsx and KanbanView.tsx no longer receive 30+ props; Dashboard.tsx routes between views. The data-model changes (SubtaskKey, ownership, workspaceId) land in Stage 3b on a clean foundation.

The success test: `wc -l src/components/Dashboard.tsx` ≤ 400 after Step 3.3 completes (we accept up to 400 here; Stage 3b will finish the reduction to ~200 once hooks take over the last polling block).

### Extraction order (DO IN THIS ORDER — commit after each step)

#### Step 3.1 — EphemeralContext (lowest risk — UI-only state move)

**File:** `src/lib/contexts/EphemeralContext.tsx` — NEW

Provider state:
- `reactOpen: string|null`, `setReactOpen`
- `commentOpen: string|null`, `setCommentOpen`
- `copied: string|null`, `setCopied`
- `hoverStage: string|null`, `setHoverStage`
- `editModeStage: string|null`, `setEditModeStage`
- `modalState: { type: string; payload?: unknown } | null`, `setModalState` (for ModalRoot in Step 3.3)

These are pure UI flicker state. No persistence, no API calls, no theming.

Hook: `useEphemeral()` returns the full bag. Components destructure what they need.

**Migration:** sweep Dashboard.tsx for the matching `useState`s, remove from Dashboard, wrap the dashboard tree in `<EphemeralProvider>`, and convert call sites in Stage.tsx, KanbanView.tsx, etc. to `useEphemeral()`.

**Done when:** Stage.tsx no longer accepts `reactOpen` / `setReactOpen` / similar as props. Dashboard.tsx is shorter by these `useState`s. Existing UI behavior unchanged.

#### Step 3.2 — ModelContext (read views + handlers)

**File:** `src/lib/contexts/ModelContext.tsx` — NEW

Provider value:
```ts
{
  pipelines, stages, reactions, comments, subtasks,
  statusOverrides, descOverrides, nameOverrides, users, currentUser,
  theme: t,
  syncStatus,   // "connecting" | "live" | "offline" — from Dashboard's existing useState
  handlers: {
    handleClaim, handleReact, handleStatusChange, addComment,
    addSubtask, toggleSubtask, lockSubtask, removeSubtask,
    archiveStage, archivePipeline, setStageDescOverride, setStageNameOverride,
    // ... all other handlers Dashboard currently defines
  }
}
```

ALL handlers Dashboard currently defines move INTO the provider. The provider owns model state in this step (still using existing `lsGet/lsSet` + `patchState`; these are replaced by useSync in Stage 3b).

Specialized selectors: `useStage(stageId)`, `useOwnership(stageId)`, `usePipeline(pipelineId)` — memoized via `useMemo` per slice.

Hook: `useModel()` returns the full value.

**Migration:** Stage.tsx and KanbanView.tsx stop receiving claims/reactions/comments/subtasks/handlers as props. They pull from `useModel()` / `useStage(id)`.

**Done when:** Stage.tsx prop interface drops from ~30 props to ~5 (`stageId`, `pipelineId`, `t`, `idx`, `expS`/expand callback). Dashboard.tsx no longer threads handler functions through children.

**NOTE on existing sync state:**
- `isInitializedRef` (useRef, line ~372 in Dashboard.tsx): keep in ModelContext provider, it guards the effect that avoids writing stale localStorage over API on mount. It does NOT move to useSync — that happens in Stage 3b.
- `isHydrating` (useState, line ~383): keep in Dashboard.tsx for now; Stage 3b's useSync replaces it.
- `syncStatus` ("connecting"|"live"|"offline", useState, line ~382): move into ModelContext value so views can read it. The polling setInterval stays in Dashboard until Stage 3b.

#### Step 3.3 — Dashboard split into route components

**New files in `src/components/views/`:**
- `PipelinesView.tsx` — pipeline-list rendering currently in Dashboard.tsx
- `ChatView.tsx` — wraps the existing ChatPanel as a routed view
- `ActivityView.tsx` — wraps ActivityFeed as a routed view
- `ArchiveView.tsx` — NEW — renders `archivedStages[]`, `archivedPipelines[]`, `archivedSubtasks[]` from ModelContext. Each item shows what it was, when archived, with an "unarchive" affordance. If no archived data, show `// nothing archived yet`.

**Existing views (already split out — verify they consume ModelContext):**
- `src/components/HomeView.tsx` — already exists. Refactor to consume `useModel()` for any props currently threaded from Dashboard.
- `src/components/TasksView.tsx` — already exists. Same.
- `src/components/KanbanView.tsx` — already exists. Same.

**`src/components/ChromeShell.tsx`** — NEW
- Renders LeftSidebar + top header bar. Header contains ThemePicker (extract from Dashboard), NotificationBell, UserMenu (extract avatar/sign-out cluster from Dashboard).

**`src/components/ModalRoot.tsx`** — NEW
- Reads `modalState` from `useEphemeral()`. Renders WelcomeModal, WorkspaceAdmin modal based on `modalState.type`.

**`src/components/Dashboard.tsx`** — reduced
- Wraps `<EphemeralProvider><ModelProvider><ChromeShell><RouteSwitch/><ModalRoot/></ChromeShell></ModelProvider></EphemeralProvider>`.
- `RouteSwitch` is a tiny switch on `activeNavItem` rendering the appropriate `*View`.
- The polling `setInterval(poll, 5000)` block stays here for now (Stage 3b replaces it).
- `isHydrating` stays here for now (Stage 3b replaces it).
- Target after this step: ≤ 400 lines. If over 500, stop and escalate before proceeding to Stage 3b.

**Done when:** `wc -l src/components/Dashboard.tsx` ≤ 400. Each `*View.tsx` is independently readable. No view component imports another view.

### Out of scope (save for Stage 3b)
- Do NOT implement useOptimisticMutation, useSync, SubtaskKey, ownership map, or workspaceId migration.
- Do NOT rename or create `/api/state` route.
- Do NOT add `archived: { stages, pipelines, subtasks }` shape to PipelineState — ArchiveView in this stage just reads the existing `archivedStages[]` arrays.

### Acceptance criteria
- `wc -l src/components/Dashboard.tsx` ≤ 400.
- `grep -rn "useState" src/components/Dashboard.tsx | wc -l` ≤ 15 (only routing, sync, hydrating state remain).
- `src/lib/contexts/EphemeralContext.tsx` and `src/lib/contexts/ModelContext.tsx` exist and are consumed.
- Stage.tsx prop count ≤ 8 (excluding implicit theme via ModelContext).
- No `reactOpen` / `setReactOpen` / `commentOpen` / `setCommentOpen` props on Stage.tsx.
- `npx tsc --noEmit` passes. `npm run build` succeeds.
- Existing user flows: claim, react, comment, drag, status change, chat, docs, search all work end-to-end.

### What QA verifies
1. Dashboard loads. `// connecting to command...` typewriter shows, then state hydrates. syncStatus dot turns green.
2. Claim a stage — ClaimChip updates. React — reaction updates. Comment — comment appears.
3. Archive a stage — ArchiveView shows it. Unarchive — it returns.
4. Mobile: sidebar hidden, BottomSheet panels functional.
5. `wc -l src/components/Dashboard.tsx` ≤ 400.
6. No TypeScript errors.

### Notes for DEV
- Commit after each step (3.1, 3.2, 3.3). Three commits. Each one is independently revertable.
- If Step 3.2 leaves Dashboard.tsx over 600 lines, the ModelContext migration is incomplete — do NOT proceed to 3.3 until all handler threads are gone from Dashboard.
- After Stage 3a ships, update `.claude/PROJECT_CONTEXT.md`: add entries for EphemeralContext and ModelContext under §0 codebase map.

+      "id": "stage-3b-batch-c-hooks-migration",
+      "status": "pending",
+      "spec": "## Stage 3b — Batch C Part 2: hooks + data-model migration

### Why this will be good
Stage 3a left Dashboard.tsx routing over named views with context providers. This stage installs the final pieces: useOptimisticMutation turns all writes into one-liner rollback-safe operations; useSync replaces the hand-rolled polling loop with a typed state machine; SubtaskKey makes stringly-typed `::` patterns unrepresentable; ownership map unifies claims; workspaceId migration removes the `pipelineIds` array inversion. After this stage, Dashboard.tsx is ~200 lines and the state machine is explicit.

The success test: `wc -l src/components/Dashboard.tsx` ≤ 250. `setInterval` no longer appears in Dashboard. Network tab shows mostly 304 responses after initial load.

### Execution order (commit after each step)

#### Step 3.4 — useOptimisticMutation hook

**File:** `src/lib/hooks/useOptimisticMutation.ts` — NEW

Signature:
```ts
useOptimisticMutation<TSlice, TArgs>(
  applyLocal: (slice: TSlice, args: TArgs) => TSlice,
  push: (args: TArgs) => Promise<void>
) => (args: TArgs) => Promise<void>
```

Behavior:
- Applies `applyLocal` immediately to local state via the appropriate ModelContext setter.
- Awaits `push`. On reject: rollback (re-apply prior slice) + show toast `// sync failed — retrying`.
- Internal queue: if `push` is in-flight, subsequent calls coalesce (last-write-wins on same slice key) rather than queue serially.

**Migration:** convert `addComment`, `handleReact`, `handleClaim` inside ModelContext to use this hook. Each handler body becomes 1–3 lines.

**Done when:** exactly one rollback path per mutation type; the three handlers above are 1–3 lines each.

#### Step 3.5 — useSync hook + state machine + delta-sync server route

**File:** `src/lib/hooks/useSync.ts` — NEW

State machine (replaces `isHydrating` + `syncStatus` + `isInitializedRef` in Dashboard/ModelContext):
```
"hydrating" → (first successful GET) → "live"
"live" ↔ (poll fail) → "offline" → (success) → "live"
"live" | "offline" → (4xx) → "error" (toast, stays until reload)
```

**Explicitly replaces these variables (NOTE: Stage 3a moved all of these from Dashboard into ModelContext.tsx):**
- `isHydrating` (useState in Dashboard.tsx line ~126) → `status === "hydrating"` from useSync (Dashboard drops this useState)
- `syncStatus` ("connecting"|"live"|"offline", ModelContext.tsx line ~200; exposed on context value; Dashboard reads it from context) → `status` returned by useSync, passed through ModelContext value
- `isInitializedRef` (useRef in ModelContext.tsx line ~203 — already removed from Dashboard by Stage 3a) → internal to useSync
- The `setInterval(poll, 5000)` block (ModelContext.tsx line ~400 — already removed from Dashboard by Stage 3a) → useSync's internal polling loop

Polling behavior:
- `live`: polls `GET /api/pipeline-state?since=<lastUpdatedAt>` every 8s (±1s jitter)
- `offline`: retries every 2s, backoff capped at 30s on repeated failure
- Returns `{ status, lastSyncAt, manualSync }`

The `// connecting to command...` typewriter (already in Dashboard from Stage 2) maps to `status === "hydrating"`.

**Server-side delta sync — extend existing GET `/api/pipeline-state`:**
- Add `?since=<ISO>` query param support.
- If `since` is provided and `state.updatedAt <= since`: return `304 Not Modified` (empty body).
- Otherwise return `{ patch: <state>, updatedAt }` as JSON.
- Verify `timestamps: true` is set on PipelineState schema (Mongoose auto-updates `updatedAt` on save). If missing, add it.
- Preserve existing GET (no `?since=`) shape — no breaking change.
- Do NOT create a new `/api/state` route — extend `/api/pipeline-state`.

**Migration in ModelContext/Dashboard:**
- Call `useSync(fetchState, patchState)` inside ModelContext provider (the polling hook belongs here alongside the data). Pass its `status` on ModelContext value (replacing the current `syncStatus` field).
- Remove `syncStatus` useState + `isInitializedRef` useRef + `setInterval(poll, 5000)` block from ModelContext.tsx.
- Remove `isHydrating` useState from Dashboard.tsx (already derives from `syncStatus` from context — update to `status === "hydrating"`).
- `isInitializedRef` is already gone from Dashboard.tsx (Stage 3a moved it) — no action needed there.

**Done when:** ModelContext.tsx has no `setInterval(fetchState...)`, no `syncStatus` useState, no `isInitializedRef` useRef. Dashboard.tsx has no `isHydrating` useState. Network tab shows 304s after initial load.

#### Step 3.6 — SubtaskKey branded type + ownership map + workspaceId migration (LAST)

**SubtaskKey branded type — `src/lib/types/SubtaskKey.ts` (NEW):**
```ts
type SubtaskKey = string & { readonly __brand: "SubtaskKey" }
namespace SubtaskKey {
  function make(stageRef: string, id: string): SubtaskKey  // "${stageRef}::${id}", validates no "::" in either part
  function parse(key: SubtaskKey): { stageRef: string; id: string }
}
```
The `::` format stays as the serialized MongoDB/localStorage form. Only CLIENT-SIDE creation and parsing are typed. Replace every `${stage}::${subtaskId}` string literal in business logic with `SubtaskKey.make(...)`. TypeScript strict mode catches remaining raw strings.

**Ownership map — replace `claims`:**
- New shape: `ownership: Record<string, { claimedBy: string[]; assignedTo?: string }>` (key is stageRef).
- Replaces both `claims: Record<string, string[]>` AND `assignments: Record<string, string>` everywhere in ModelContext state.
- NOTE: Stage 3a already added `useOwnership(stageId)` selector in ModelContext (line ~761) that returns `{ claimers, isMine, assignedTo, isAssignedToMe }`. Update this selector to read from the new `ownership` map — the selector's return shape stays identical, so consumers are unaffected.
- `handleClaim` and `assignTask` handlers write to `ownership` map; external API (function signatures) unchanged.
- Migration on hydrate (one-time in ModelContext): if old `claims` shape present, fold into `ownership` (set `claimedBy = claims[stage]`, `assignedTo = assignments[stage]`), write back via PATCH, clear `claims` and `assignments`.
- Server (`validate.ts`): add `ownership` to `ALLOWED_PATCH_KEYS` whitelist. Keep `claims` and `assignments` in whitelist for the migration write-back.

**pipelines own workspaceId — 3-step migration:**
- (a) Add `workspaceId?: string` field to `Pipeline` type in `src/lib/data.ts`. Static pipeline definitions do NOT need a hardcoded value — the migration sets it at runtime.
- (b) Migration on hydrate (one-time in ModelContext): for each workspace in `workspaces[]`, read `workspace.pipelineIds[]`, and for each id, find the matching pipeline and set `pipeline.workspaceId = workspace.id` in state. If a pipeline has no `pipelineIds` entry (legacy/missing), assign it to the singleton default workspace. Write updated pipelines back via PATCH (`pipelineWorkspaceMap: Record<string, string>` — a lightweight id→workspaceId map stored in PipelineState, NOT the full pipeline objects). Add `pipelineWorkspaceMap` to `ALLOWED_PATCH_KEYS`.
- (c) After migration: use `pipelines.filter(p => p.workspaceId === wsId)` instead of `workspace.pipelineIds`. Drop `pipelineIds` from the `Workspace` type in `data.ts` only after the migration helper confirms all pipelines have a workspaceId.

**Archive map — structured shape for ArchiveView:**
- New PipelineState shape: `archived: { stages: ArchivedItem[]; pipelines: ArchivedItem[]; subtasks: ArchivedItem[] }`.
- `ArchivedItem = { id: string; archivedAt: number; archivedBy: string; payload: unknown }`.
- Migration on hydrate: fold existing `archivedStages: string[]` into `archived.stages[]` (set `id`, `archivedAt = 0`, `archivedBy = ""`, `payload = null` for legacy items). Same for archivedPipelines, archivedSubtasks.
- ArchiveView (from Stage 3a) now reads `archived.*` instead of flat arrays.
- Add `archived` to `ALLOWED_PATCH_KEYS`.

**Done when:** no raw `${stage}::${id}` string in business logic; `claims` replaced by `ownership`; `workspace.pipelineIds` gone from Workspace type; `archived` is a structured map; `wc -l src/components/Dashboard.tsx` ≤ 250.

### Out of scope
- Do not introduce Redux/Zustand/Jotai.
- Do not rename `/api/pipeline-state`.
- Do not migrate ChatPanel's local state into ModelContext.

### Acceptance criteria
- `wc -l src/components/Dashboard.tsx` ≤ 250.
- `grep -rn "useState" src/components/Dashboard.tsx | wc -l` ≤ 5 (routing-level only).
- `useSync` is the only polling primitive. No `setInterval(fetchState...)` in Dashboard or ModelContext.
- `isHydrating` useState gone from Dashboard.tsx. `syncStatus` useState + `isInitializedRef` useRef gone from ModelContext.tsx.
- `useOptimisticMutation` used by addComment, handleReact, handleClaim. Each handler 1–3 lines.
- `SubtaskKey.make()` replaces all raw `::` concatenations in business logic.
- `ownership` replaces `claims`; migration is silent on fresh load with old data.
- `pipelines[].workspaceId` set at runtime; `workspace.pipelineIds` absent from Workspace type.
- `archived` is structured map; ArchiveView reads from it.
- `GET /api/pipeline-state?since=<ISO>` returns 304 when state is unchanged.
- `npx tsc --noEmit` passes. `npm run build` succeeds.

### Why this will be good
Stage 3a left Dashboard.tsx routing over named views with context providers. This stage installs the final pieces: useOptimisticMutation turns all writes into one-liner rollback-safe operations; useSync replaces the hand-rolled polling loop with a typed state machine; SubtaskKey makes stringly-typed `::` patterns unrepresentable; ownership map unifies claims; workspaceId migration removes the `pipelineIds` array inversion. After this stage, Dashboard.tsx is ~200 lines and the state machine is explicit.

The success test: `wc -l src/components/Dashboard.tsx` ≤ 250. `setInterval` no longer appears in Dashboard. Network tab shows mostly 304 responses after initial load.

### Execution order (commit after each step)

#### Step 3.4 — useOptimisticMutation hook

**File:** `src/lib/hooks/useOptimisticMutation.ts` — NEW

Signature:
```ts
useOptimisticMutation<TSlice, TArgs>(
  applyLocal: (slice: TSlice, args: TArgs) => TSlice,
  push: (args: TArgs) => Promise<void>
) => (args: TArgs) => Promise<void>
```

Behavior:
- Applies `applyLocal` immediately to local state via the appropriate ModelContext setter.
- Awaits `push`. On reject: rollback (re-apply prior slice) + show toast `// sync failed — retrying`.
- Internal queue: if `push` is in-flight, subsequent calls coalesce (last-write-wins on same slice key) rather than queue serially.

**Migration:** convert `addComment`, `handleReact`, `handleClaim` inside ModelContext to use this hook. Each handler body becomes 1–3 lines.

**Done when:** exactly one rollback path per mutation type; the three handlers above are 1–3 lines each.

#### Step 3.5 — useSync hook + state machine + delta-sync server route

**File:** `src/lib/hooks/useSync.ts` — NEW

State machine (replaces `isHydrating` + `syncStatus` + `isInitializedRef` in Dashboard/ModelContext):
```
"hydrating" → (first successful GET) → "live"
"live" ↔ (poll fail) → "offline" → (success) → "live"
"live" | "offline" → (4xx) → "error" (toast, stays until reload)
```

**Explicitly replaces these variables (NOTE: Stage 3a moved all of these from Dashboard into ModelContext.tsx):**
- `isHydrating` (useState in Dashboard.tsx line ~126) → `status === "hydrating"` from useSync (Dashboard drops this useState)
- `syncStatus` ("connecting"|"live"|"offline", ModelContext.tsx line ~200; exposed on context value; Dashboard reads it from context) → `status` returned by useSync, passed through ModelContext value
- `isInitializedRef` (useRef in ModelContext.tsx line ~203 — already removed from Dashboard by Stage 3a) → internal to useSync
- The `setInterval(poll, 5000)` block (ModelContext.tsx line ~400 — already removed from Dashboard by Stage 3a) → useSync's internal polling loop

Polling behavior:
- `live`: polls `GET /api/pipeline-state?since=<lastUpdatedAt>` every 8s (±1s jitter)
- `offline`: retries every 2s, backoff capped at 30s on repeated failure
- Returns `{ status, lastSyncAt, manualSync }`

The `// connecting to command...` typewriter (already in Dashboard from Stage 2) maps to `status === "hydrating"`.

**Server-side delta sync — extend existing GET `/api/pipeline-state`:**
- Add `?since=<ISO>` query param support.
- If `since` is provided and `state.updatedAt <= since`: return `304 Not Modified` (empty body).
- Otherwise return `{ patch: <state>, updatedAt }` as JSON.
- Verify `timestamps: true` is set on PipelineState schema (Mongoose auto-updates `updatedAt` on save). If missing, add it.
- Preserve existing GET (no `?since=`) shape — no breaking change.
- Do NOT create a new `/api/state` route — extend `/api/pipeline-state`.

**Migration in ModelContext/Dashboard:**
- Call `useSync(fetchState, patchState)` inside ModelContext provider (the polling hook belongs here alongside the data). Pass its `status` on ModelContext value (replacing the current `syncStatus` field).
- Remove `syncStatus` useState + `isInitializedRef` useRef + `setInterval(poll, 5000)` block from ModelContext.tsx.
- Remove `isHydrating` useState from Dashboard.tsx (already derives from `syncStatus` from context — update to `status === "hydrating"`).
- `isInitializedRef` is already gone from Dashboard.tsx (Stage 3a moved it) — no action needed there.

**Done when:** ModelContext.tsx has no `setInterval(fetchState...)`, no `syncStatus` useState, no `isInitializedRef` useRef. Dashboard.tsx has no `isHydrating` useState. Network tab shows 304s after initial load.

#### Step 3.6 — SubtaskKey branded type + ownership map + workspaceId migration (LAST)

**SubtaskKey branded type — `src/lib/types/SubtaskKey.ts` (NEW):**
```ts
type SubtaskKey = string & { readonly __brand: "SubtaskKey" }
namespace SubtaskKey {
  function make(stageRef: string, id: string): SubtaskKey  // "${stageRef}::${id}", validates no "::" in either part
  function parse(key: SubtaskKey): { stageRef: string; id: string }
}
```
The `::` format stays as the serialized MongoDB/localStorage form. Only CLIENT-SIDE creation and parsing are typed. Replace every `${stage}::${subtaskId}` string literal in business logic with `SubtaskKey.make(...)`. TypeScript strict mode catches remaining raw strings.

**Ownership map — replace `claims`:**
- New shape: `ownership: Record<string, { claimedBy: string[]; assignedTo?: string }>` (key is stageRef).
- Replaces both `claims: Record<string, string[]>` AND `assignments: Record<string, string>` everywhere in ModelContext state.
- NOTE: Stage 3a already added `useOwnership(stageId)` selector in ModelContext (line ~761) that returns `{ claimers, isMine, assignedTo, isAssignedToMe }`. Update this selector to read from the new `ownership` map — the selector's return shape stays identical, so consumers are unaffected.
- `handleClaim` and `assignTask` handlers write to `ownership` map; external API (function signatures) unchanged.
- Migration on hydrate (one-time in ModelContext): if old `claims` shape present, fold into `ownership` (set `claimedBy = claims[stage]`, `assignedTo = assignments[stage]`), write back via PATCH, clear `claims` and `assignments`.
- Server (`validate.ts`): add `ownership` to `ALLOWED_PATCH_KEYS` whitelist. Keep `claims` and `assignments` in whitelist for the migration write-back.

**pipelines own workspaceId — 3-step migration:**
- (a) Add `workspaceId?: string` field to `Pipeline` type in `src/lib/data.ts`. Static pipeline definitions do NOT need a hardcoded value — the migration sets it at runtime.
- (b) Migration on hydrate (one-time in ModelContext): for each workspace in `workspaces[]`, read `workspace.pipelineIds[]`, and for each id, find the matching pipeline and set `pipeline.workspaceId = workspace.id` in state. If a pipeline has no `pipelineIds` entry (legacy/missing), assign it to the singleton default workspace. Write updated pipelines back via PATCH (`pipelineWorkspaceMap: Record<string, string>` — a lightweight id→workspaceId map stored in PipelineState, NOT the full pipeline objects). Add `pipelineWorkspaceMap` to `ALLOWED_PATCH_KEYS`.
- (c) After migration: use `pipelines.filter(p => p.workspaceId === wsId)` instead of `workspace.pipelineIds`. Drop `pipelineIds` from the `Workspace` type in `data.ts` only after the migration helper confirms all pipelines have a workspaceId.

**Archive map — structured shape for ArchiveView:**
- New PipelineState shape: `archived: { stages: ArchivedItem[]; pipelines: ArchivedItem[]; subtasks: ArchivedItem[] }`.
- `ArchivedItem = { id: string; archivedAt: number; archivedBy: string; payload: unknown }`.

- ArchiveView (from Stage 3a) now reads `archived.*` instead of flat arrays.
- Add `archived` to `ALLOWED_PATCH_KEYS`.

**Done when:** no raw `${stage}::${id}` string in business logic; `claims` replaced by `ownership`; `workspace.pipelineIds` gone from Workspace type; `archived` is a structured map; `wc -l src/components/Dashboard.tsx` ≤ 250.

+"use client";
+
+import React from "react";
+
+interface ChromeShellProps {
+  sidebar: React.ReactNode;
+  header: React.ReactNode;
+  children: React.ReactNode;
+  outerStyle?: React.CSSProperties;
+  mainStyle?: React.CSSProperties;
+  headerStyle?: React.CSSProperties;
+  contentStyle?: React.CSSProperties;
+}
+
+/**
+ * ChromeShell — pure layout wrapper.
+ * Renders: sidebar (sticky left) + right column (header on top, children below).
+ * Reads nothing from context — all layout concerns passed as props.
+ */
+export function ChromeShell({
+  sidebar,
+  header,
+  children,
+  outerStyle,
+  mainStyle,
+  headerStyle,
+  contentStyle,
+}: ChromeShellProps) {
+  return (
+    <div style={{ display: "flex", flexDirection: "row", ...outerStyle }}>
+      {sidebar}
+      <div style={{ flex: 1, minWidth: 0, display: "flex", flexDirection: "column", overflowX: "hidden", ...mainStyle }}>
+        <div style={headerStyle}>{header}</div>
+        <div style={{ flex: 1, minWidth: 0, overflowX: "hidden", ...contentStyle }}>
+          {children}
+        </div>
+      </div>
+    </div>
+  );
+}
+
+export default ChromeShell;

+"use client";
+
+import { Suspense } from "react";
+import { useModel } from "@/lib/contexts/ModelContext";
+import { ErrorBoundary } from "@/components/ui/ErrorBoundary";
+import { ActivitySkeleton } from "@/components/ui/Skeletons";
+import dynamic from "next/dynamic";
+
+const ActivityFeed = dynamic(() => import("@/components/ActivityFeed"), { ssr: false });
+
+export default function ActivityView({ showToast }: { showToast: (msg: string, color: string) => void }) {
+  const { activityLog, users, t } = useModel();
+  return (
+    <ErrorBoundary onError={() => showToast("// failed to load panel — refresh to retry", t.red)}>
+      <Suspense fallback={<ActivitySkeleton t={t} />}>

+      </Suspense>
+    </ErrorBoundary>
+  );
+}

new file mode 100644

+++ b/src/components/views/ArchiveView.tsx
@@ -0,0 +1,46 @@
+"use client";
+
+import { useModel } from "@/lib/contexts/ModelContext";
+
+export default function ArchiveView() {
+  const { archivedStages, archivedPipelines, stageNameOverrides, pipeMetaOverrides, allPipelinesGlobal, restoreStage, restorePipeline, t } = useModel();
+
+  return (
+    <div style={{ display: "flex", flexDirection: "column", gap: 12 }}>
+      <div style={{ display: "flex", gap: 8, alignItems: "center", marginBottom: 8 }}>
+        <div style={{ fontSize: 13, fontWeight: 800, color: t.text }}>📦 archive</div>
+      </div>
+      {archivedStages.length === 0 && archivedPipelines.length === 0 ? (
+        <div style={{ fontSize: 11, color: t.textDim, fontFamily: "var(--font-dm-mono), monospace" }}>// nothing archived yet</div>
+      ) : (
+        <>
+          {archivedStages.length > 0 && (
+            <div>
+              <div style={{ fontSize: 10, color: t.textMuted, fontWeight: 700, letterSpacing: 0.5, textTransform: "uppercase", fontFamily: "var(--font-dm-mono), monospace", marginBottom: 6 }}>tasks ({archivedStages.length})</div>
+              {archivedStages.map(sid => (
+                <div key={sid} style={{ display: "flex", alignItems: "center", gap: 8, padding: "6px 8px", borderRadius: 8, background: t.bgHover, marginBottom: 4 }}>
+                  <span style={{ flex: 1, fontSize: 12, color: t.text, overflow: "hidden", textOverflow: "ellipsis", whiteSpace: "nowrap" }}>{stageNameOverrides[sid] || sid}</span>
+                  <button onClick={() => restoreStage(sid)} style={{ background: t.green + "18", border: `1px solid ${t.green}44`, borderRadius: 6, padding: "2px 8px", cursor: "pointer", fontSize: 10, color: t.green, fontWeight: 700, fontFamily: "var(--font-dm-mono), monospace" }}>restore</button>
+                </div>
+              ))}
+            </div>
+          )}
+          {archivedPipelines.length > 0 && (
+            <div>
+              <div style={{ fontSize: 10, color: t.textMuted, fontWeight: 700, letterSpacing: 0.5, textTransform: "uppercase", fontFamily: "var(--font-dm-mono), monospace", marginBottom: 6 }}>pipelines ({archivedPipelines.length})</div>
+              {archivedPipelines.map(pid => {
+                const p = allPipelinesGlobal.find(p => p.id === pid);
+                return (
+                  <div key={pid} style={{ display: "flex", alignItems: "center", gap: 8, padding: "6px 8px", borderRadius: 8, background: t.bgHover, marginBottom: 4 }}>
+                    <span style={{ flex: 1, fontSize: 12, color: t.text }}>{p?.icon} {pipeMetaOverrides[pid]?.name || p?.name || pid}</span>
+                    <button onClick={() => restorePipeline(pid)} style={{ background: t.green + "18", border: `1px solid ${t.green}44`, borderRadius: 6, padding: "2px 8px", cursor: "pointer", fontSize: 10, color: t.green, fontWeight: 700, fontFamily: "var(--font-dm-mono), monospace" }}>restore</button>
+                  </div>
+                );
+              })}
+            </div>
+          )}
+        </>
+      )}
+    </div>
+  );
+}
diff --git a/src/components/views/ChatView.tsx b/src/components/views/ChatView.tsx
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..83fdb4d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/components/views/ChatView.tsx
@@ -0,0 +1,48 @@
+"use client";
+
+import { Suspense } from "react";
+import { useModel } from "@/lib/contexts/ModelContext";
+import { ErrorBoundary } from "@/components/ui/ErrorBoundary";
+import { ChatSkeleton } from "@/components/ui/Skeletons";
+import dynamic from "next/dynamic";
+
+const ChatPanel = dynamic(() => import("@/components/ChatPanel"), { ssr: false });
+
+interface ChatViewProps {
+  showToast: (msg: string, color: string) => void;
+  fullScreen?: boolean;
+  defaultTab?: "team" | "ai";
+  currentWorkspaceId?: string;
+}
+
+export default function ChatView({ showToast, fullScreen, defaultTab, currentWorkspaceId }: ChatViewProps) {
+  const { chatMessages, sendChat, handleRemoteMessage, users, currentUser, hasMoreMessages, loadMoreMessages, allPipelinesGlobal, customPipelines, customStages, pipeMetaOverrides, pipeDescOverrides, claims, subtasks, comments, stageDescOverrides, activityLog, getStatus, getPoints, workspaces, t } = useModel();
+
+  const allPipelines = currentWorkspaceId
+    ? (() => { const ws = workspaces.find(w => w.id === currentWorkspaceId); return ws ? allPipelinesGlobal.filter(p => ws.pipelineIds.includes(p.id)) : allPipelinesGlobal; })()
+    : allPipelinesGlobal;
+
+  const buildAiContext = () => {
+    const me = users.find(u => u.id === currentUser);
+    const lines: string[] = [];
+    lines.push(`Current user: ${me?.name || currentUser} (id=${currentUser}, role=${me?.role || "?"}, points=${getPoints(currentUser!)})`);
+    lines.push(`Team: ${users.map(u => `${u.name} (${u.id}, ${u.role}, ${getPoints(u.id)}pts)`).join("; ")}`);
+    lines.push(""); lines.push(`Pipelines (${allPipelines.length}):`);
+    allPipelines.forEach((p, pi) => {
+      const stages = [...p.stages, ...(customStages[p.id] || [])];
+      lines.push(`${pi + 1}. ${pipeMetaOverrides[p.id]?.name || p.name} — ${pipeMetaOverrides[p.id]?.priority || p.priority} — ${pipeDescOverrides[p.id] || p.desc}`);
+      stages.forEach((s, si) => { const st = getStatus(s); const claimers = (claims[s] || []).map(id => users.find(u => u.id === id)?.name || id).join(", ") || "unclaimed"; const subN = (subtasks[s] || []).length; const comN = (comments[s] || []).length; const sDesc = stageDescOverrides[s] || ""; lines.push(`   ${pi + 1}.${si + 1} ${s} [${st}] — claimed by ${claimers}${subN ? ` — subtasks ${subN}` : ""}${comN ? ` — ${comN} comments` : ""}${sDesc ? ` — ${sDesc}` : ""}`); });
+    });
+    const recent = activityLog.slice(0, 8);
+    if (recent.length) { lines.push(""); lines.push("Recent activity:"); recent.forEach(a => lines.push(`- ${a.user} ${a.type} ${a.target}${a.detail ? ` (${a.detail})` : ""}`)); }
+    return lines.join("\n");
+  };
+
+  return (
+    <ErrorBoundary onError={() => showToast("// failed to load panel — refresh to retry", t.red)}>
+      <Suspense fallback={<ChatSkeleton t={t} />}>
+        <ChatPanel fullScreen={fullScreen} messages={chatMessages} onSend={sendChat} onRemoteMessage={handleRemoteMessage} users={users} currentUser={currentUser!} t={t} defaultTab={defaultTab || "team"} onLoadMore={loadMoreMessages} hasMore={hasMoreMessages} buildAiContext={buildAiContext} />
+      </Suspense>
+    </ErrorBoundary>
+  );
+}
diff --git a/src/components/views/HomeViewRoute.tsx b/src/components/views/HomeViewRoute.tsx
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..462c9cb
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/components/views/HomeViewRoute.tsx
@@ -0,0 +1,98 @@
+"use client";
+
+import { Suspense } from "react";
+import { useModel } from "@/lib/contexts/ModelContext";
+import { ErrorBoundary } from "@/components/ui/ErrorBoundary";
+import dynamic from "next/dynamic";
+import { AvatarC } from "@/components/ui/Avatar";
+import { signOut } from "next-auth/react";
+import { MessageSquare, Bell } from "lucide-react";
+import type { NavItem } from "@/components/LeftSidebar";
+
+const HomeView = dynamic(() => import("@/components/HomeView"), { ssr: false });
+
+interface HomeViewRouteProps {
+  showToast: (msg: string, color: string) => void;
+  currentWorkspaceId: string;
+  setCurrentWorkspaceId: (id: string) => void;
+  setActiveSidebarPipeline: (id: string | null) => void;
+  setActiveNavItem: (item: NavItem) => void;
+  viewingUser: string | null;
+  setViewingUser: (id: string | null) => void;
+  showActivity: boolean;
+  setShowActivity: (v: boolean) => void;
+  setLastSeenActivity: (n: number) => void;
+  showThemePicker: boolean;
+  setShowThemePicker: (v: boolean) => void;
+  selUser: string | null;
+  setSelUser: (id: string | null) => void;
+  selAvatar: string | null;
+  setSelAvatar: (v: string | null) => void;
+  setShowAvatarPicker: (v: boolean) => void;
+  handleClaimWithAnim: (sid: string) => void;
+  editMode?: boolean;
+  unseen: number;
+  themeId: string;
+  isDark: boolean;
+  setThemeId: (v: string) => void;
+  setIsDark: (v: boolean) => void;
+}
+
+export default function HomeViewRoute({
+  showToast, currentWorkspaceId, setCurrentWorkspaceId, setActiveSidebarPipeline,
+  setActiveNavItem, viewingUser, setViewingUser, showActivity, setShowActivity,
+  setLastSeenActivity, showThemePicker, setShowThemePicker, selUser, setSelUser,
+  selAvatar, setSelAvatar, setShowAvatarPicker, handleClaimWithAnim,
+  editMode, unseen, themeId, isDark, setThemeId, setIsDark,
+}: HomeViewRouteProps) {
+  const {
+    users, currentUser, me, allPipelinesGlobal,
+    pipeMetaOverrides, workspaces, activityLog, getPoints, t,
+  } = useModel();
+
+  const myWorkspaces = workspaces.filter(w => currentUser ? w.members.includes(currentUser!) : true);
+  const isCaptainOfAny = !!currentUser && workspaces.some(w => w.captains.includes(currentUser!));
+
+  const hBtn: React.CSSProperties = { display: "flex", alignItems: "center", justifyContent: "center", background: t.bgCard, border: `1px solid ${t.border}`, borderRadius: 12, padding: "0 12px", cursor: "pointer", color: t.textMuted, fontFamily: "var(--font-dm-mono), monospace", fontSize: 11, fontWeight: 600, whiteSpace: "nowrap" as const, gap: 4, minHeight: 44 };
+
+  if (!me) return null;
+  return (
+    <ErrorBoundary onError={() => showToast("// home failed to load — refresh to retry", t.red)}>
+      <Suspense fallback={null}>
+        <HomeView
+          t={t}
+          me={me}
+          users={users}
+          myWorkspaces={myWorkspaces}
+          allPipelinesGlobal={allPipelinesGlobal}
+          pipeMetaOverrides={pipeMetaOverrides}
+          currentUser={currentUser!}
+          isCaptainOfAny={isCaptainOfAny}
+          currentWorkspaceId={currentWorkspaceId}
+          onSwitchWorkspace={(id) => { setCurrentWorkspaceId(id); setActiveSidebarPipeline(null); }}
+          editMode={editMode}
+          onPipelineClick={(pid) => { setActiveNavItem("pipelines"); setActiveSidebarPipeline(pid); }}
+          onUserClick={(uid) => setViewingUser(viewingUser === uid ? null : uid)}
+          navbarSlot={(
+            <div style={{ display: "flex", alignItems: "center", gap: 4 }}>
+              <div onClick={e => { e.stopPropagation(); setSelUser(currentUser); setSelAvatar(me.avatar); setShowAvatarPicker(true); }} style={{ display: "flex", alignItems: "center", gap: 8, background: t.bgCard, border: `1px solid ${t.border}`, borderRadius: 12, padding: "6px 10px", cursor: "pointer" }} onMouseEnter={e => (e.currentTarget as HTMLElement).style.borderColor = me.color + "55"} onMouseLeave={e => (e.currentTarget as HTMLElement).style.borderColor = t.border}>
+                <AvatarC user={me} size={24} />
+                <div>
+                  <div style={{ fontSize: 12, fontWeight: 800, color: t.text }}>{me.name}</div>
+                  <div style={{ fontSize: 10, color: t.accent, fontWeight: 700, fontFamily: "var(--font-dm-mono), monospace" }}>{getPoints(currentUser!)}pts</div>
+                </div>
+              </div>
+              <button onClick={e => { e.stopPropagation(); setActiveNavItem("chat"); }} style={{ ...hBtn, fontSize: 14 }}><MessageSquare size={14} strokeWidth={1.8} /></button>
+              <button onClick={e => { e.stopPropagation(); setShowActivity(!showActivity); if (!showActivity) setLastSeenActivity(activityLog.length); }} style={{ ...hBtn, fontSize: 14, position: "relative" }}>
+                <Bell size={14} strokeWidth={1.8} />
+                {unseen > 0 && <div style={{ position: "absolute", top: 4, right: 4, minWidth: 12, height: 12, borderRadius: 8, background: t.red, border: `2px solid ${t.bg}`, display: "flex", alignItems: "center", justifyContent: "center", fontSize: 10, color: "#fff", fontWeight: 800 }}>{unseen > 9 ? "9+" : unseen}</div>}
+              </button>
+              <button onClick={() => setShowThemePicker(!showThemePicker)} style={{ ...hBtn, fontSize: 14, gap: 3 }}>{t.icon} <span style={{ fontSize: 11 }}>▾</span></button>
+              <button onClick={() => signOut({ callbackUrl: "/login" })} style={{ ...hBtn, fontSize: 11 }}>sign out</button>
+            </div>
+          )}
+        />
+      </Suspense>
+    </ErrorBoundary>
+  );
+}

+"use client";

+import { Suspense, useState } from "react";

+import dynamic from "next/dynamic";
+import { REACTIONS } from "@/lib/data";
+
+const TasksView = dynamic(() => import("@/components/TasksView"), { ssr: false });
+const OverviewPanel = dynamic(() => import("@/components/OverviewPanel"), { ssr: false });
+
+const ICON_OPTIONS = ["🔧", "🚀", "💡", "🎯", "⚡", "🔥", "🤖", "💥", "✨", "📊"];
+const PRIORITY_CYCLE = ["NOW", "HIGH", "MEDIUM", "LOW"] as const;
+const COLOR_OPTIONS = ["blue", "purple", "green", "amber", "cyan", "red", "orange", "lime", "slate"] as const;
+
+interface PipelinesViewProps {
+  view: "list" | "kanban" | "overview";
+  setView: (v: "list" | "kanban" | "overview") => void;
+  expanded: string[];
+  setExpanded: React.Dispatch<React.SetStateAction<string[]>>;
+  expS: string | null;
+  setExpS: (v: string | null) => void;
+  searchQ: string;
+  setSearchQ: (v: string) => void;
+  statusFilter: string | null;
+  setStatusFilter: (v: string | null) => void;
+  claimAnim: { stage: string; pts: number } | null;
+  subtaskInput: Record<string, string>;
+  setSubtaskInput: React.Dispatch<React.SetStateAction<Record<string, string>>>;
+  commentInput: Record<string, string>;
+  setCommentInput: React.Dispatch<React.SetStateAction<Record<string, string>>>;
+  showMockup: Record<string, boolean>;
+  setShowMockup: React.Dispatch<React.SetStateAction<Record<string, boolean>>>;
+  isMobile: boolean;

+            <div style={{ display: "flex", gap: 4, marginBottom: 12, flexWrap: "wrap", alignItems: "center" }}>
+              <span style={{ fontSize: 10, color: t.textDim, fontFamily: "var(--font-dm-mono), monospace" }}>PRIORITY:</span>
+              {PRIORITY_CYCLE.map(p => <button key={p} onClick={() => setNewPipeForm(prev => ({ ...prev, priority: p }))} style={{ background: newPipeForm.priority === p ? (pr[p]?.c || t.accent) + "22" : "transparent", border: `1px solid ${newPipeForm.priority === p ? (pr[p]?.c || t.accent) + "55" : t.border}`, borderRadius: 8, padding: "4px 8px", cursor: "pointer", fontSize: 10, color: newPipeForm.priority === p ? pr[p]?.c || t.accent : t.textMuted, fontWeight: 700, fontFamily: "var(--font-dm-mono), monospace" }}>{p}</button>)}
+              <span style={{ fontSize: 10, color: t.textDim, fontFamily: "var(--font-dm-mono), monospace", marginLeft: 8 }}>COLOR:</span>
+              {COLOR_OPTIONS.map(c => <div key={c} onClick={() => setNewPipeForm(p => ({ ...p, colorKey: c }))} style={{ width: 14, height: 14, borderRadius: "50%", background: ck[c], cursor: "pointer", border: newPipeForm.colorKey === c ? `2px solid ${t.text}` : "2px solid transparent", flexShrink: 0 }} />)}
+            </div>
+            <div style={{ display: "flex", gap: 8 }}>
+              <button onClick={addCustomPipelineLocal} disabled={!newPipeForm.name.trim()} style={{ background: t.accent, border: "none", borderRadius: 12, padding: "8px 20px", cursor: newPipeForm.name.trim() ? "pointer" : "not-allowed", fontSize: 13, color: "#fff", fontWeight: 800, fontFamily: "var(--font-dm-mono), monospace", opacity: newPipeForm.name.trim() ? 1 : 0.45, transition: "opacity 0.15s" }}>create pipeline</button>
+              <button onClick={() => setAddingPipeline(false)} style={{ background: "transparent", border: `1px solid ${t.border}`, borderRadius: 12, padding: "8px 16px", cursor: "pointer", fontSize: 13, color: t.textMuted, fontFamily: "var(--font-dm-mono), monospace" }}>cancel</button>
+            </div>
+          </div>
+        )}
+      </div>}
+    </div>
+  );
+}
diff --git a/src/components/views/TeamBar.tsx b/src/components/views/TeamBar.tsx
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..23b31f9
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/components/views/TeamBar.tsx
@@ -0,0 +1,74 @@
+"use client";
+
+import { useRef } from "react";
+import { useModel } from "@/lib/contexts/ModelContext";
+import { AvatarC } from "@/components/ui/Avatar";
+import NotificationPrefs from "@/components/NotificationPrefs";
+
+interface TeamBarProps {
+  ptsFlash: boolean;
+  viewingUser: string | null;
+  setViewingUser: (id: string | null) => void;
+  currentWorkspaceId: string;
+  onAvatarClick: (userId: string, avatar: string) => void;
+}
+
+export default function TeamBar({ ptsFlash, viewingUser, setViewingUser, currentWorkspaceId, onAvatarClick }: TeamBarProps) {
+  const { users, currentUser, claims, getStatus, getPoints, workspaces, t } = useModel();
+  const popupRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
+  const currentWorkspace = workspaces.find(w => w.id === currentWorkspaceId) || null;
+
+  const userRankInCurrent = (uid: string): "captain" | "firstMate" | "crew" | null => {
+    if (!currentWorkspace) return null;
+    if (currentWorkspace.captains.includes(uid)) return "captain";
+    if (currentWorkspace.firstMates.includes(uid)) return "firstMate";
+    if (currentWorkspace.members.includes(uid)) return "crew";
+    return null;
+  };
+
+  return (
+    <div className="bu-team" style={{ display: "flex", alignItems: "center", gap: 16, marginBottom: 16, padding: "12px 16px", background: t.bgCard, border: `1px solid ${t.border}`, borderRadius: 16, flexWrap: "wrap" }}>
+      {users.map(u => {
+        const isMe = u.id === currentUser;
+        const uPts = getPoints(u.id);
+        const claimedStages = Object.entries(claims).filter(([, cl]) => (cl as string[]).includes(u.id)).map(([s]) => s);
+        const rank = [...users].sort((a, b) => getPoints(b.id) - getPoints(a.id)).findIndex(x => x.id === u.id) + 1;
+        const rankEmoji = rank === 1 ? "🥇" : rank === 2 ? "🥈" : rank === 3 ? "🥉" : `#${rank}`;
+        return (
+          <div key={u.id} style={{ position: "relative" }}>
+            <div onClick={e => { e.stopPropagation(); setViewingUser(viewingUser === u.id ? null : u.id); }} style={{ display: "flex", alignItems: "center", gap: 4, cursor: "pointer", borderRadius: 12, padding: "4px 4px", margin: "-4px -6px" }} onMouseEnter={e => { (e.currentTarget as HTMLElement).style.background = u.color + "12"; }} onMouseLeave={e => { (e.currentTarget as HTMLElement).style.background = "transparent"; }}>
+              <div style={{ borderRadius: "50%", padding: isMe ? 2 : 0, background: isMe ? `linear-gradient(135deg,${u.color},${u.color}88)` : "transparent", flexShrink: 0, position: "relative" }}>
+                <AvatarC user={u} size={26} />
+                {userRankInCurrent(u.id) === "captain" && <span style={{ position: "absolute", bottom: -2, right: -4, fontSize: 13, filter: "drop-shadow(0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.4))" }}>👑</span>}
+                {userRankInCurrent(u.id) === "firstMate" && <span style={{ position: "absolute", bottom: -2, right: -4, fontSize: 13, filter: "drop-shadow(0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.4))" }}>⚓</span>}
+              </div>
+              <div>
+                <div style={{ fontSize: 11, fontWeight: isMe ? 900 : 800, color: isMe ? u.color : t.text }}>{u.name}</div>
+                <div style={{ fontSize: 10, color: uPts > 0 ? t.accent : t.textDim, fontFamily: "var(--font-dm-mono), monospace", animation: isMe && ptsFlash ? "ptsCount 0.6s ease" : "none" }}>{uPts}pts</div>
+              </div>
+            </div>
+            {viewingUser === u.id && (
+              <div ref={popupRef} onClick={e => e.stopPropagation()} style={{ position: "absolute", top: "calc(100% + 10px)", left: 0, zIndex: 200, background: t.bgCard, border: `1.5px solid ${u.color}44`, borderRadius: 16, padding: "16px", minWidth: 210, maxWidth: "min(320px, calc(100vw - 32px))", boxShadow: t.shadowLg, animation: "fadeIn 0.15s ease" }}>
+                <button onClick={() => setViewingUser(null)} style={{ position: "absolute", top: 10, right: 10, background: "none", border: "none", cursor: "pointer", fontSize: 15, color: t.textDim, padding: "0 4px", borderRadius: 8 }}>×</button>
+                <div style={{ display: "flex", gap: 8, alignItems: "center", marginBottom: 12 }}><div style={{ borderRadius: "50%", background: `linear-gradient(135deg,${u.color},${u.color}66)` }}><AvatarC user={u} size={42} /></div><div><div style={{ fontSize: 15, fontWeight: 900, color: u.color }}>{u.name} <span style={{ fontSize: 13 }}>{rankEmoji}</span></div><div style={{ fontSize: 11, color: t.textMuted, fontFamily: "var(--font-dm-mono), monospace" }}>{u.role}</div></div></div>
+                <div style={{ display: "grid", gridTemplateColumns: "1fr 1fr", gap: 4, marginBottom: 8 }}>
+                  {[{ val: uPts, label: "pts", color: t.accent }, { val: claimedStages.length, label: "owned", color: t.accent }].map(({ val, label, color }) => (
+                    <div key={label} style={{ background: t.bgHover, borderRadius: 12, padding: "8px 4px", textAlign: "center" }}><div style={{ fontSize: 16, fontWeight: 900, color, fontFamily: "var(--font-dm-mono), monospace", animation: isMe && ptsFlash && label === "pts" ? "ptsCount 0.6s ease" : "none" }}>{val}</div><div style={{ fontSize: 10, color: t.textDim, textTransform: "uppercase", letterSpacing: 0.5 }}>{label}</div></div>
+                  ))}
+                </div>
+                {claimedStages.length > 0 && (
+                  <div style={{ marginBottom: isMe ? 10 : 0 }}>
+                    <div style={{ fontSize: 10, color: t.textDim, letterSpacing: 0.5, textTransform: "uppercase", marginBottom: 4, fontWeight: 600 }}>owned stages</div>
+                    {claimedStages.map(s => { const st = { active: t.green, "in-progress": t.amber, planned: t.cyan || t.accent, concept: t.purple }[getStatus(s)] || "#888"; return (<div key={s} style={{ display: "flex", alignItems: "center", gap: 4, fontSize: 10, color: t.textSec, marginBottom: 2 }}><span style={{ width: 6, height: 6, borderRadius: "50%", background: st, flexShrink: 0, boxShadow: getStatus(s) === "active" ? `0 0 6px ${st}` : "none" }} /><span style={{ flex: 1, overflow: "hidden", textOverflow: "ellipsis", whiteSpace: "nowrap" }}>{s}</span></div>); })}
+                  </div>
+                )}
+                {claimedStages.length === 0 && <div style={{ fontSize: 10, color: t.textDim, fontStyle: "italic", marginBottom: isMe ? 10 : 0 }}>no stages claimed yet</div>}
+                {isMe && (<><button onClick={() => { onAvatarClick(u.id, u.avatar); setViewingUser(null); }} style={{ width: "100%", background: u.color + "18", border: `1px solid ${u.color}44`, borderRadius: 12, padding: "8px", cursor: "pointer", fontSize: 11, color: u.color, fontWeight: 700, fontFamily: "var(--font-dm-mono), monospace", textAlign: "center" }}>change avatar →</button><NotificationPrefs t={t} /></>)}
+              </div>
+            )}
+          </div>
+        );
+      })}
+    </div>
+  );
+}
diff --git a/src/lib/contexts/EphemeralContext.tsx b/src/lib/contexts/EphemeralContext.tsx
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..d5eb5da
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/lib/contexts/EphemeralContext.tsx
@@ -0,0 +1,33 @@
+"use client";
+import { createContext, useContext, useState, type ReactNode } from "react";
+
+interface EphemeralContextValue {
+  reactOpen: string | null; setReactOpen: (v: string | null) => void;
+  commentOpen: string | null; setCommentOpen: (v: string | null) => void;
+  assignOpen: string | null; setAssignOpen: (v: string | null) => void;
+  copied: string | null; setCopied: (v: string | null) => void;
+  hoverStage: string | null; setHoverStage: (v: string | null) => void;
+  editModeStage: string | null; setEditModeStage: (v: string | null) => void;
+}
+
+const EphemeralContext = createContext<EphemeralContextValue | null>(null);
+
+export function EphemeralProvider({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) {
+  const [reactOpen, setReactOpen] = useState<string | null>(null);
+  const [commentOpen, setCommentOpen] = useState<string | null>(null);
+  const [assignOpen, setAssignOpen] = useState<string | null>(null);
+  const [copied, setCopied] = useState<string | null>(null);
+  const [hoverStage, setHoverStage] = useState<string | null>(null);
+  const [editModeStage, setEditModeStage] = useState<string | null>(null);
+  return (
+    <EphemeralContext.Provider value={{ reactOpen, setReactOpen, commentOpen, setCommentOpen, assignOpen, setAssignOpen, copied, setCopied, hoverStage, setHoverStage, editModeStage, setEditModeStage }}>
+      {children}
+    </EphemeralContext.Provider>
+  );
+}
+
+export function useEphemeral() {
+  const ctx = useContext(EphemeralContext);
+  if (!ctx) throw new Error("useEphemeral must be used within EphemeralProvider");
+  return ctx;
+}
diff --git a/src/lib/contexts/ModelContext.tsx b/src/lib/contexts/ModelContext.tsx
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..ce5e5dd
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/lib/contexts/ModelContext.tsx
@@ -0,0 +1,739 @@
+"use client";
+
+import {
+  createContext, useContext, useState, useEffect, useCallback, useRef, type ReactNode,
+} from "react";
+import { lsGet, lsSet } from "@/lib/storage";
+import {
+  pipelineData, stageDefaults, USERS_DEFAULT, STATUS_ORDER,
+  ADMIN_IDS, DEFAULT_WORKSPACE_ID,
+  type UserType, type SubtaskItem, type CommentItem, type ActivityItem, type Workspace,
+} from "@/lib/data";
+import { mkTheme, type T } from "@/lib/themes";
+import { fetchState, patchState, pushMessage, pushComment, pushActivity } from "@/lib/apiSync";
+import { type ChatMsg } from "@/components/ChatPanel";
+
+export type CustomPipeline = {
+  id: string; name: string; desc: string; icon: string;
+  colorKey: string; priority: string; totalHours: string; points: number; stages: string[];
+};
+
+// Always take name/role/avatar/color from USERS_DEFAULT — only preserve aiAvatar from saved state
+export function hydrateUsers(saved: UserType[], current: UserType[] = []): UserType[] {
+  const savedMap = Object.fromEntries(saved.map(u => [u.id, u]));
+  const currentMap = Object.fromEntries(current.map(u => [u.id, u]));
+  return USERS_DEFAULT.map(def => ({
+    ...def,
+    avatar: currentMap[def.id]?.avatar || savedMap[def.id]?.avatar || "",
+    aiAvatar: currentMap[def.id]?.aiAvatar || savedMap[def.id]?.aiAvatar,
+  })) as UserType[];
+}
+
+const PRIORITY_CYCLE = ["NOW", "HIGH", "MEDIUM", "LOW"] as const;
+
+interface ModelContextValue {
+  // Users / identity
+  users: UserType[];
+  setUsers: React.Dispatch<React.SetStateAction<UserType[]>>;
+  currentUser: string | null;
+  setCurrentUser: React.Dispatch<React.SetStateAction<string | null>>;
+  me: UserType | undefined;
+
+  // Model state
+  claims: Record<string, string[]>;
+  reactions: Record<string, Record<string, string[]>>;
+  comments: Record<string, CommentItem[]>;
+  subtasks: Record<string, SubtaskItem[]>;
+  assignments: Record<string, string>;
+  stageStatusOverrides: Record<string, string>;
+  approvedStages: string[];
+  stageDescOverrides: Record<string, string>;
+  stageNameOverrides: Record<string, string>;
+  subtaskStages: Record<string, string>;
+  pipeDescOverrides: Record<string, string>;
+  setPipeDescOverrides: React.Dispatch<React.SetStateAction<Record<string, string>>>;
+  pipeMetaOverrides: Record<string, { name?: string; priority?: string }>;
+  setPipeMetaOverrides: React.Dispatch<React.SetStateAction<Record<string, { name?: string; priority?: string }>>>;
+  customStages: Record<string, string[]>;
+  customPipelines: CustomPipeline[];
+  workspaces: Workspace[];
+  setWorkspaces: React.Dispatch<React.SetStateAction<Workspace[]>>;
+  activityLog: ActivityItem[];
+  archivedStages: string[];
+  archivedPipelines: string[];
+  archivedSubtasks: string[];
+  stageImages: Record<string, string[]>;
+
+  // Chat state
+  chatMessages: ChatMsg[];
+  setChatMessages: React.Dispatch<React.SetStateAction<ChatMsg[]>>;
+  hasMoreMessages: boolean;
+  chatNotif: { name: string; text: string; isComment?: boolean; stage?: string; isReaction?: boolean; isClaim?: boolean } | null;
+  setChatNotif: React.Dispatch<React.SetStateAction<{ name: string; text: string; isComment?: boolean; stage?: string; isReaction?: boolean; isClaim?: boolean } | null>>;
+
+  // Live notifs (in-place stage card notification bursts)
+  liveNotifs: Record<string, { comment?: string; reaction?: string }>;
+
+  // Sync status
+  syncStatus: "connecting" | "live" | "offline";
+
+  // Computed
+  getStatus: (name: string) => string;
+  getPoints: (uid: string) => number;
+  sc: Record<string, { l: string; c: string }>;
+  ck: Record<string, string>;
+  pr: Record<string, { c: string }>;
+
+  // All pipelines (static + custom)
+  allPipelinesGlobal: (typeof pipelineData[number] | CustomPipeline)[];
+
+  // Handlers
+  handleClaim: (sid: string) => void;
+  handleReact: (sid: string, emoji: string) => void;
+  addComment: (sid: string, val: string, clearInput: () => void) => void;
+  addSubtask: (sid: string, val: string, clearInput: () => void) => void;
+  toggleSubtask: (sid: string, taskId: number) => void;
+  renameSubtask: (sid: string, taskId: number, text: string) => void;
+  lockSubtask: (sid: string, taskId: number) => void;
+  removeSubtask: (sid: string, taskId: number) => void;
+  archiveStage: (sid: string) => void;
+  restoreStage: (sid: string) => void;
+  archivePipeline: (pid: string) => void;
+  restorePipeline: (pid: string) => void;
+  archiveSubtask: (key: string) => void;
+  restoreSubtask: (key: string) => void;
+  setStageDescOverride: (name: string, val: string) => void;
+  setStageNameOverride: (name: string, val: string) => void;
+  setSubtaskStage: (key: string, status: string) => void;
+  assignTask: (sid: string, userId: string | null) => void;
+  setStageStatusDirect: (name: string, status: string) => void;
+  cycleStatus: (name: string) => void;
+  approveStage: (name: string) => void;
+  addCustomStage: (pid: string, val: string) => void;
+  addCustomPipeline: (form: { name: string; desc: string; icon: string; colorKey: string; priority: string }) => string | null;
+  cyclePriority: (pid: string, cur: string) => void;
+  addStageImage: (sid: string, dataUrl: string) => void;
+  removeStageImage: (sid: string, idx: number) => void;
+  sendChat: (text: string) => void;
+  handleRemoteMessage: (msg: ChatMsg) => void;
+  loadMoreMessages: () => Promise<void>;
+  logActivity: (type: string, target: string, detail: string) => void;
+
+  // Workspace handlers
+  createWorkspace: (name: string, icon: string, colorKey: string) => void;
+  addMemberToWorkspace: (workspaceId: string, userId: string) => void;
+  removeMemberFromWorkspace: (workspaceId: string, userId: string) => void;
+  setMemberRank: (workspaceId: string, userId: string, rank: "captain" | "firstMate" | "crew") => void;
+  deleteWorkspace: (workspaceId: string) => void;
+  isOfficerOfWorkspace: (workspaceId: string) => boolean;
+
+  // Theme (re-exported so consumers don't need separate theme prop)
+  t: T;
+}
+
+const ModelContext = createContext<ModelContextValue | null>(null);
+
+interface ModelProviderProps {
+  children: ReactNode;
+  initialUserId?: string;
+  themeId: string;
+  isDark: boolean;
+  showToast?: (msg: string, color: string) => void;
+  currentWorkspaceId: string;
+}
+
+// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-empty-function
+const noopToast = () => {};
+
+export function ModelProvider({
+  children,
+  initialUserId,
+  themeId,
+  isDark,
+  showToast = noopToast,
+  currentWorkspaceId,
+}: ModelProviderProps) {
+  const t = mkTheme(themeId, isDark);
+
+  // ── Identity ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+  const [currentUser, setCurrentUser] = useState<string | null>(() => {
+    if (initialUserId) return initialUserId;
+    return lsGet("currentUser", null);
+  });
+  const [users, setUsers] = useState(() => hydrateUsers(lsGet("users", []) as UserType[]));
+
+  useEffect(() => {
+    if (initialUserId) {
+      lsSet("currentUser", initialUserId);
+      setCurrentUser(initialUserId);
+    }
+  // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps
+  }, []);
+
+  // ── Model state ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+  const [reactions, setReactions] = useState<Record<string, Record<string, string[]>>>(() => lsGet("reactions", {}));
+  const [claims, setClaims] = useState<Record<string, string[]>>(() => lsGet("claims", {}));
+  const [assignments, setAssignments] = useState<Record<string, string>>(() => lsGet("assignments", {}));
+  const [subtasks, setSubtasks] = useState<Record<string, SubtaskItem[]>>(() => lsGet("subtasks", {}));
+  const [comments, setComments] = useState<Record<string, CommentItem[]>>(() => lsGet("comments", {}));
+  const [stageStatusOverrides, setStageStatusOverrides] = useState<Record<string, string>>(() => lsGet("stageStatusOverrides", {}));
+  const [approvedStages, setApprovedStages] = useState<string[]>(() => lsGet("approvedStages", []));
+  const [stageDescOverrides, setStageDescOverrides] = useState<Record<string, string>>(() => lsGet("stageDescOverrides", {}));
+  const [stageNameOverrides, setStageNameOverrides] = useState<Record<string, string>>(() => lsGet("stageNameOverrides", {}));
+  const [subtaskStages, setSubtaskStages] = useState<Record<string, string>>(() => lsGet("subtaskStages", {}));
+  const [pipeDescOverrides, setPipeDescOverrides] = useState<Record<string, string>>(() => lsGet("pipeDescOverrides", {}));
+  const [pipeMetaOverrides, setPipeMetaOverrides] = useState<Record<string, { name?: string; priority?: string }>>(() => lsGet("pipeMetaOverrides", {}));
+  const [customStages, setCustomStages] = useState<Record<string, string[]>>(() => lsGet("customStages", {}));
+  const [customPipelines, setCustomPipelines] = useState<CustomPipeline[]>(() => lsGet("customPipelines", []));
+  const [workspaces, setWorkspaces] = useState<Workspace[]>(() => lsGet("workspaces", []));
+  const [activityLog, setActivityLog] = useState<ActivityItem[]>(() => lsGet("activityLog", []));
+  const [archivedStages, setArchivedStages] = useState<string[]>(() => lsGet("archivedStages", []));
+  const [archivedPipelines, setArchivedPipelines] = useState<string[]>(() => lsGet("archivedPipelines", []));
+  const [archivedSubtasks, setArchivedSubtasks] = useState<string[]>(() => lsGet("archivedSubtasks", []));
+  const [stageImages, setStageImages] = useState<Record<string, string[]>>(() => lsGet("stageImages", {}));
+
+  // ── Chat state ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+  const [chatMessages, setChatMessages] = useState<ChatMsg[]>([]);
+  const [hasMoreMessages, setHasMoreMessages] = useState(true);
+  const [chatNotif, setChatNotif] = useState<{ name: string; text: string; isComment?: boolean; stage?: string; isReaction?: boolean; isClaim?: boolean } | null>(null);
+  const [liveNotifs, setLiveNotifs] = useState<Record<string, { comment?: string; reaction?: string }>>({});
+  const [syncStatus, setSyncStatus] = useState<"connecting" | "live" | "offline">("connecting");
+
+  // ── Sync refs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
+  const isInitializedRef = useRef(false);
+  const isPollUpdateRef = useRef(false);
+  const lastWriteRef = useRef<number>(0);
+  const syncTimerRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null>(null);
+  const knownCommentsRef = useRef<Record<string, number>>({});
+  const prevClaimsRef = useRef<Record<string, string[]>>({});
+  const prevReactionsRef = useRef<Record<string, Record<string, string[]>>>({});
+
+  // ── LocalStorage persistence ──────────────────────────────────────────────
+  useEffect(() => { lsSet("currentUser", currentUser) }, [currentUser]);
+  useEffect(() => { lsSet("users", users) }, [users]);
+  useEffect(() => { lsSet("reactions", reactions) }, [reactions]);
+  useEffect(() => { lsSet("claims", claims) }, [claims]);
+  useEffect(() => { lsSet("assignments", assignments) }, [assignments]);
+  useEffect(() => { lsSet("subtasks", subtasks) }, [subtasks]);
+  useEffect(() => { lsSet("comments", comments) }, [comments]);
+  useEffect(() => { lsSet("stageStatusOverrides", stageStatusOverrides) }, [stageStatusOverrides]);
+  useEffect(() => { lsSet("approvedStages", approvedStages) }, [approvedStages]);
+  useEffect(() => { lsSet("stageImages", stageImages) }, [stageImages]);
+  useEffect(() => { lsSet("stageDescOverrides", stageDescOverrides) }, [stageDescOverrides]);
